frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre bhai, ambition is fine, but when children come, priorities have to change. Father can support, of course, but maa ka role cannot be outsourced to daycare and maids. Aaj kal people act as if both careers are equal at that stage, but bachpan mein bachche ko sabse zyada maa hi chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as primarily the mother's responsibility while treating the father's role as secondary support. It encodes career sacrifice framing and guilt around daycare/outsourcing care.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
